skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.summary: Creates a compact summary of the current conversation for a fresh AI agent to continue the work seamlessly.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 4 static findings: 2 high-severity weak crypto warnings are clear false positives (lines contain descriptions and argument handling, not crypto). The medium-severity external_commands finding is a false positive (backtick usage in markdown references mktemp as an instruction, not code execution). The low-severity filesystem finding is a true positive but reflects expected behavior: the skill writes a markdown handoff document to a temp file via mktemp, which is standard and low risk. No prompt injection attempts detected. No data exfiltration, network access, or env access patterns found.

low_findings.title: Temp file creation via mktemp
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create and write a handoff document to a temp file using mktemp. This is expected behavior for a handoff document skill. The use of mktemp ensures secure temp file creation with random names. Risk is limited to writing markdown text content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed filesystem interaction at line 7. However, mktemp is a standard safe pattern for temp file creation and the written content is markdown text, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Shell command reference in markdown (false positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick usage around mktemp as shell execution. However, this is markdown backtick formatting referencing a command by name as an instruction to the agent. The agent would need to execute mktemp to function, which is legitimate behavior for this skill type.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High confidence this is a false positive. The backticks are markdown formatting, not actual shell execution. No command injection or dynamic argument construction present.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3 (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 for weak crypto. Line 3 is the YAML front matter description field. No cryptographic algorithm, key generation, or encoding present. This is a false match by the scanner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very high confidence this is a false positive. Line 3 contains only 'description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.' No cryptographic content exists.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm at line 13 (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 13 for weak crypto. Line 13 handles user arguments for focus area descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, encoding, or key material present. This is a false match by the scanner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very high confidence this is a false positive. Line 13 contains only 'If the user passed arguments, treat them as a description of what the next session will focus on and tailor the doc accordingly.' No cryptographic content exists.

content.user_title: Hand off your conversation to another AI agent
content.value_statement: Long AI sessions lose context over time as conversations grow. This skill creates a compact handoff document that lets a fresh agent continue your work without repeating yourself.
actual_capabilities: Creates a handoff document that summarizes the current conversation for a fresh agent
Generates a temp file path using mktemp and writes the handoff to it
References existing artifacts (PRDs, plans, ADRs, commits, diffs) by path instead of duplicating content
Tailors the handoff content based on user-specified focus area arguments
Suggests relevant skills for the next session to use
Produces a compact document designed for immediate action by a new agent
limitations: Cannot transfer in-memory state such as unsaved variables or active processes to the next session
Requires the new agent to have filesystem access to read the handoff file
Cannot guarantee the new agent will interpret the handoff content identically to the original
Only captures information explicitly discussed in the current conversation

use_cases.title: Hand off a debugging session to a colleague
use_cases.description: After investigating a bug for hours, create a handoff document so another developer can pick up where you left off without re-reading the entire conversation.
use_cases.target_user: Software developer switching tasks or shifts

use_cases.title: Resume a stalled feature implementation
use_cases.description: When a complex feature implementation is interrupted, save a handoff that captures design decisions, progress, and next steps.
use_cases.target_user: Developer returning to a project after a break

use_cases.title: Transfer research findings to a planning session
use_cases.description: After researching technical options, create a handoff document that summarizes findings for use in a subsequent architecture planning session.

prompt_templates.title: Simple session handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the current conversation into a handoff document so a fresh agent can continue.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user wanting to preserve basic conversation context before ending a session

prompt_templates.title: Handoff with artifact references
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff document for the current session. Reference existing PRDs, plans, and commits by path. Do not duplicate their content.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to minimize duplication by linking to existing artifacts

prompt_templates.title: Focused handoff for a specific next task
prompt_templates.prompt: This session will focus on implementing the payment module. Create a handoff document that covers the payment flow design decisions, the API contracts discussed, and the remaining implementation steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a clear goal for the next session and wants the handoff tailored to that goal

prompt_templates.title: Handoff with skill recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a handoff document for this coding session. Include references to all relevant artifacts. Suggest which skills the next session should load and what their initial focus should be.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user configuring an optimal starting state for the next agent session

output_examples.input: Summarize the current debugging session into a handoff document.
output: A handoff document describing the bug, all attempted fixes and their results, remaining hypotheses about the root cause, and recommended next debugging steps.
References to relevant error logs and stack traces by file path.
Suggested skills for the next session: debugging tools and log analysis skills.

output_examples.input: Hand off the feature implementation with focus on testing strategy.
output: A handoff document focused on the testing approach: unit test structure, integration test scenarios, edge cases identified, and mocking strategy.
References to the PRD and implementation plan by path.
A checklist of remaining testing tasks ordered by priority.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff for the code review session tomorrow.
output: A handoff document listing all files changed in this branch, key design decisions made and their rationale, areas that need reviewer attention, and open questions for the reviewer.
References to the diff and commit history by URL.
Suggested review order from most to least critical files.

best_practices: Run this skill before ending a session to preserve full context for future work.
Reference existing artifacts by path or URL to keep the handoff document compact and avoid duplication.
Use the argument feature to describe the next session focus for a more relevant handoff document.
anti_patterns: Copying entire conversation transcripts into the handoff document instead of writing a concise summary.
Duplicating content already captured in other artifacts such as PRDs, plans, or commits.
Starting a new session without running the handoff first, which loses valuable context.

faq.question: What is a handoff document?
faq.answer: A handoff document is a compact summary of your current conversation that helps a new AI agent continue your work without repeating the entire discussion.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all AI tools?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can I specify what the next session should focus on?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass an argument when invoking the skill to describe the focus area for the next session.

faq.question: Will the handoff document include my source code?
faq.answer: No. The handoff references existing artifacts by path or URL instead of duplicating code or content.

faq.question: Where is the handoff document saved?
faq.answer: The handoff document is saved as a temp file on your filesystem using mktemp.

faq.question: Does the handoff document work with any AI agent?
faq.answer: The document is designed for AI agents that can read files and follow natural language instructions.
